info.title: Regesta HTTP API
info.summary: Machine-readable reference for the current Regesta HTTP surface.
info.description: This document describes implemented core registry routes and the current npm projection routes. Regesta-native object shapes are referenced from the JSON Schema reference. Protocol semantics remain defined by the TypeScript protocol and core validation code.

servers.description: Experimental core registry API demo host

tags.name: Transport
tags.description: HTTP mechanics such as host routing, CORS, deployment info, health, and readiness.

tags.name: Core Registry
tags.description: Regesta-native packages, releases, channels, objects, and events.

tags.name: npm Projection
tags.description: npm-compatible projection over Regesta-native package data, with optional upstream fallback for missing packages. Fallback metadata requests forward only metadata negotiation and cache validators; fallback metadata responses expose only cache and content metadata headers.

get.summary: Read host-specific root information

get.summary: Read npm scoped packument or unscoped package version
get.description: This physical npm route has two native npm meanings: `@scope/name` reads a scoped packument, while `name/tag` or `name/version` reads an unscoped fallback package version manifest, such as `/tinyexec/latest`.

head.summary: Read npm scoped packument or unscoped package version headers
head.description: This physical npm route has two native npm meanings: `@scope/name` reads scoped packument headers, while `name/tag` or `name/version` reads unscoped fallback package version headers.

AcceptRangesBytes.description: Server supports byte-range requests for this object.

ContentRange.description: Byte range selected for a partial response, or unsatisfied range size for 416 responses.

ContentLength.description: Decimal byte length of the response body or selected byte range.

ImmutableCacheControl.description: Immutable representation can be cached long-term.

FaviconCacheControl.description: Browser favicon probe response can be cached briefly.

NoCacheControl.description: Mutable representation must be revalidated before reuse.

NoStoreCacheControl.description: Response must not be stored by caches.

RootCacheControl.description: Core registry root responses use no-store; npm projection root responses use no-cache.

NpmMetadataCacheControl.description: Cache policy for npm metadata. Local mutable projections use no-cache, local immutable version responses can be cached long-term, and upstream fallback metadata preserves upstream cache policy without forwarding upstream cookies, redirects, authentication challenges, or extension headers.

NpmMetadataEtag.description: Validator for npm metadata. Local projections derive this from Regesta state; upstream fallback metadata preserves upstream validators without forwarding upstream cookies, redirects, authentication challenges, or extension headers.

NpmMetadataLastModified.description: HTTP date validator for npm metadata when available, especially from upstream fallback metadata. It is part of the fallback response metadata allowlist.

NpmMetadataIfModifiedSince.name: If-Modified-Since
NpmMetadataIfModifiedSince.description: Client timestamp validator for npm metadata. Local projections can use it when Last-Modified is available and If-None-Match is not present; upstream fallback forwards it to the upstream npm registry as part of the request header allowlist.

NpmMetadataIfNoneMatch.name: If-None-Match
NpmMetadataIfNoneMatch.description: Client ETag validator for npm metadata. Local projections can return 304 when it matches the current ETag; upstream fallback forwards it to the upstream npm registry as part of the request header allowlist.

IfNoneMatch.name: If-None-Match
IfNoneMatch.description: Client ETag validator. Routes with an ETag can return 304 when this matches the current representation.

NpmName.name: name
NpmName.description: npm path segment. For scoped package routes this is the package name. For unscoped fallback version routes under `/{scope}/{name}`, this segment is the requested tag or version.

NpmScope.name: scope
NpmScope.description: npm path segment. For scoped package routes this is the leading scope, including `@`. For unscoped fallback version routes under `/{scope}/{name}`, this segment is the package name.

PackageId.name: packageId
PackageId.description: URL-encoded as one path segment, for example npm%3Asome.dev%2Fsdk.

Range.name: Range
Range.description: Single byte range for immutable object reads. Current object responses support one `bytes=start-end` range.

NpmTarballRedirect.description: Redirect direct npm projection tarball requests. Local releases redirect to immutable core object URLs; missing packages and versions redirect to the upstream npm registry tarball URL. The npm projection never serves or proxies tarball bytes.

ObjectRangeNotSatisfiable.description: Requested object byte range is not satisfiable.

ObjectNotModified.description: Client validator still matches the current immutable object representation.

ImmutableNotModified.description: Client validator still matches the current immutable representation.

ETag.description: Weak or strong validator for the immutable representation.

MutableNotModified.description: Client validator still matches the current mutable representation.

NotModified.description: Client validator still matches current representation.

statistics.description: Advisory deployment statistics for operators. Implementations may cache these values briefly to keep status checks cheap under load. The default server may serve stale cached statistics when a refresh read fails, but schema-invalid statistics still fail closed. Storage adapters should expose them from cheap counters or indexes, not by replaying events or scanning release rows on every root request.

packages.description: Approximate current package count exposed as status data, not a consistency boundary.

NpmUtilityRoot.description: Empty JSON object returned by npm projection root routes for npm client compatibility.

NpmVersionManifest.description: npm-compatible version metadata. Regesta-hosted package metadata projects only supported resolver fields from artifact metadata; upstream fallback metadata preserves upstream fields.

tarball.description: Tarball URL for npm-compatible clients. Regesta-hosted package metadata points to the npm projection tarball URL, which redirects to the immutable core object URL; fallback metadata preserves the upstream npm tarball URL without rewriting.

ReadinessStatus.description: Aggregate adapter readiness status. Checks are independent and bounded by deployment timeout policy; clients should read named check results and must not depend on probe ordering. Optional checkpoint store readiness appears when configured.
